citeinfo.title: Phytoplankton data for samples collected at eleven large river sites throughout the United States, June through October 2018
citeinfo.geoform: Excel Spreadsheet and Comma Separated Values (CSV) file

citeinfo.title: Cyanobacteria, cyanotoxin synthetase gene, and cyanotoxin occurrence among selected large river sites of the conterminous United States, 2017–18

descript.abstract: This U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) Data Release provides phytoplankton data for samples collected from eleven large river sites throughout the United States, from June through October 2018. All data are reported as raw calculated values and are not rounded to USGS significant figures. The dataset includes all routine and quality assurance/quality control samples collected as part of a National Water Quality Assessment Project pilot study to describe cyanobacteria and cyanotoxin occurrence in the Nation's large rivers. Phytoplankton were identified to the lowest possible taxonomic level and abundance (reported as both natural units and cells) is reported.
descript.purpose: These data were collected by the USGS National Water-Quality Assessment Project as part of a pilot study conducted during 2017 through 2019 to describe cyanobacteria and cyanotoxin occurrence in the Nation's large rivers. This data release was produced in compliance with the federal open-data requirements as a way to make scientific products associated with USGS research efforts and publications available to the public.

spdom.descgeog: Data were collected from eleven large river sites throughout the United States: Connecticut River at Thompsonville, CT (USGS Station Identification Number 01184000); Delaware River at Trenton, NJ (USGS Station Identification Number 01463500); Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD (USGS Station Identification Number 01578310); Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA (USGS Station Identification Number 02338000); Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN (USGS Station Identification Number 03303280); Mississippi River at Hastings, MN (USGS Station Identification Number 05331580); Kansas River at De Soto, KS (USGS Station Identification Number 06892350); Missouri River at Hermann, MO (USGS Station Identification Number 06934500); Trinity River below Dallas, TX (USGS Station Identification Number 08057410); Sacramento River at Freeport, CA (USGS Station Identification Number 11447650); and Willamette River at Portland, OR (USGS Station Identification Number 14211720).

place.placekey: Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN
Kansas River at De Soto, KS

idinfo.useconst: Public domain data from the U.S. Government are freely redistributable with proper metadata and source attribution. Please recognize the U.S. Geological Survey as the source of this information. Users are advised to read the dataset's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate use and data limitations.

cntperp.cntorg: U.S. Geological Survey, New York Water Science Center

idinfo.datacred: This study was conducted by the U.S. Geological Survey National Water Quality Assessment Project.

citeinfo.origin: Rosen, B.H., Davis, T.W., Gobler, C.J., Kramer, B.J., and Loftin. K.A.
citeinfo.title: Cyanobacteria of the 2016 Lake Okeechobee and Okeechobee Waterway harmful algal bloom

citeinfo.title: Phytoplankton tally sheet, including photomicrographs, for samples collected at eleven large river sites throughout the United States, June through September 2017

citeinfo.origin: Komarek, J., Kastovsky, J., Mares, J., and Johansen, J.R.
citeinfo.title: Taxonomic classification of cyanoprokaryotes (cyanobacterial genera) 2014, using a polyphasic approach

citeinfo.title: Understanding the effect of salinity tolerance on cyanobacteria associated with a harmful algal bloom in Lake Okeechobee, Florida

serinfo.sername: U.S. Geological Survey Scientific Investigations Report

citeinfo.origin: Wehr, J.D., Sheath, R.G., and Kociolek, J.P.
citeinfo.title: Freshwater Algae of North America: Ecology and Classification

cntperp.cntper: Barry H. Rosen, U.S. Geological Survey Emeritus

taxonsys.taxonpro: Phytoplankton were enumerated to the lowest possible taxonomic level using concentration techniques described in Rosen and others (2017 and 2018). Phytoplankton were identified by morphological traits such as dimensions (length and width) of cells, the arrangement of cells in colonies or filaments, and the terminal cell shape in a filament. The presence of specialized structures such as aerotopes (also known as gas vesicles used for regulating buoyancy), heterocytes (specialized cells for nitrogen fixation), and akinetes (specialized resting cells that allow organisms to survive harsh conditions and germinate when environmental conditions allow) also were used in the identification of cyanobacteria. The dimensions and shape of these specialized cells are critical to the identification of a cyanobacterial species. Several sources were used to identify organisms on the basis of morphology (Komarek and Anagnostidis, 1998, 2005; Komarek, 2008, 2013; Komarek and others, 2014; and Wehr and others, 2015).
taxonsys.taxoncom: Phytoplankton were enumerated to the lowest possible taxonomic level, which was at least to genus for most organisms. The photomicrographs presented in King and others (2019) were used to verify identifications and ensure taxonomic consistency between the 2017 and 2018 datasets. The phytoplankton data collected from eleven large river sites throughout the United States, during June through October 2018, contained 98 unique genera in 7 Divisions (Phyla). Taxonomic citations are included for the 7 Divisions in the dataset.

attracc.attraccr: Data were analyzed by Dr. Barry H. Rosen, whose laboratory does its own quality assurance and quality control procedures. All data were broadly checked for accuracy; no obvious outliers were present in the data.

dataqual.logic: No formal logical accuracy tests were conducted, but data were checked for naming consistency and numerical errors.
dataqual.complete: Dataset is considered complete for the information presented, as described in the abstract. Users are advised to read the rest of the metadata record carefully for additional details.

horizpa.horizpar: No formal positional accuracy tests were conducted.

vertacc.vertaccr: No formal positional accuracy tests were conducted.

procstep.procdesc: All data were compiled into one spreadsheet for this data release. Data were evaluated to ensure consistency in naming conventions and presentation of dates and times. Phytoplankton abundances as natural units per milliliter were calculated using the following equation: natural units per milliliter = tally / raw volume counted / concentration factor. A natural unit is a single cell, colony, or filament. Phytoplankton abundances as cells per milliliter were calculated using the following equation: cells per milliliter = raw cell count / raw volume counted / concentration factor. Data used to calculate abundances can be found in this data set.

method.methdesc: Discrete phytoplankton samples were collected from eleven large river sites in the United States, from June through October 2018. Samples were collected in accordance with USGS sampling protocols that follow isokinetic equal width or depth integrated techniques (U.S. Geological Survey, 2006). Replicate samples were collected from most sites, and also are included in the dataset. Replicate samples can be identified as those having timestamps 1, 5, or 10 minutes after the rountine sample collected on any given date.

serinfo.sername: U.S Geological Survey Techniques of Water Resources Investigations

spdoinfo.indspref: Connecticut River at Thompsonville, CT (01184000) Long= -72.60583 Lat= 41.98722; Delaware River at Trenton, NJ (01463500) Long= -74.77806 Lat= 40.22167; Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD (01578310) Long= -76.17444 Lat= 39.65789; Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA (02338000) Long= -84.90083 Lat= 33.47694; Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN (03303280) Long= -86.70556 Lat= 37.89944; Mississippi River at Hastings, MN (05331580) Long= -92.84778 Lat= 44.74583; Kansas River at De Soto, KS (06892350) Long= -94.96444 Lat= 38.98333; Missouri River at Hermann, MO (06934500) Long= -91.43850 Lat= 38.70981; Trinity River below Dallas, TX (08057410) Long= -96.73556 Lat= 32.70750; Sacramento River at Freeport, CA (11447650) Long= -121.5003 Lat= 38.45611; Willamette River at Portland, OR (14211720) Long= -122.6692 Lat= 45.5175

enttyp.enttypd: Taxonomic identification and abundance data for phytoplankton samples collected at eleven large river sites throughout the United States, during June through October 2018.

edom.edomvd: Connecticut River at Thompsonville; CT, Delaware River at Trenton, NJ; Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD; Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA; Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN; Mississippi River at Hastings, MN; Kansas River at De Soto, KS; Missouri River at Hermann, MO; Trinity River below Dallas, TX; Sacramento River at Freeport, CA; and Willamette River at Portland, OR.

attr.attrdef: Initial volume of preserved samples received and processed.
Weight of subsample; data are calculated raw values and are not rounded to USGS significant figures.

Number of strips counted; Based on magnification, the number of strips is defined as how many passes it takes to cover the area of a 22 mm coverslip; data are calculated raw values and are not rounded to USGS significant figures.
Volume of sample per strip; data are calculated raw values and are not rounded to USGS significant figures.
The volume of sample counted; data are calculated raw values and are not rounded to USGS significant figures.
Concentration factor describes the ratio between the original sample volume and the final concentrated volume of 10 milliliters that was subsampled for identification and enumeration.
The number of natural units (single cell, colony, or filament) of a specific taxa counted in a sample.
The number of cells of a specific taxa counted in a sample; "NM" indicates value was not measured.

edom.edomvd: One of seven phytoplankton divisions: Bacillariophyta (Ochrophyta, Bacillariophyceae), Chlorophyta, Chrysophyta, Cryptophyta (Cryptophycophyta), Cyanophyta (Cyanobacteria), Euglenophyta (Euglenophycota), or Pyrrophyta (Pyrrophycophyta).

attr.attrdef: Phytoplankton taxonomy genus and species name.

edom.edomvd: Presented as Genus or Genus species; Distinct unknown species are indicated as sp.; Genera with several distinct but unknown species are indicated as spp.; Compare with is indicated as cf.

attr.attrdef: The number of natural units (single cell, colony, or filament) per mL; data are calculated raw values and are not rounded to USGS significant figures.
The number of cells per mL; data are calculated raw values and are not rounded to USGS significant figures; "NM" indicates value was not measured.

cntaddr.address: Denver Federal Center, Building 810, Mail Stop 302

distinfo.distliab: Unless otherwise stated, all data, metadata and related materials are considered to satisfy the quality standards relative to the purpose for which the data were collected. Although these data and associated metadata have been reviewed for accuracy and completeness and approved for release by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), no warranty expressed or implied is made regarding the display or utility of the data on any other system or for general or scientific purposes, nor shall the act of distribution constitute any such warranty.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
